ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jun-26-2024 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jun-26-2024 Continued To Jul-10-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion appeared by zoom for San Francisco Public Administrator.
Leighton Burrey appeared by zoom for Darlene Daniels (former administrator).
Patricia Boyes appeared by telephone for and with Lawrence Matrin, Jr. in person and for and with Jewel Martin in person.

Mr. Burrey confirmed that the accounting was just filed. Mrs. Mannion stated that she had the opportunity to speak with both of the attorneys, and that there seems to be a resolution of sorts, but this is an unfortunate situation where the personal representative seemingly has absconded with all of the money. Mrs. Mannion also stated that she informed counsel that the Public Administrator's Office is not a debt collector, and raised the issue of the possibility of the parties filing something against Mrs. Daniels in a fiduciary capacity or an individual capacity. Mr. Burrey stated that he has no idea where Darlene is now, and that correspondence that was sent to the only address he has for her came back as return mail. Mrs. Boyes acknowledged that Darlene can't be found and the money is gone, and expressed concern about the costs and expense that would be involved in pursuing this. Status on close of estate is continued to July 10, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow the accounting to post. It is not necessary for Mr. Burrey to notice the Public Administrator or for Mrs. Mannion to appear at future hearings.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar May-15-2024 Continued To Jun-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leighton Burrey appeared by zoom for Darlene Daniels.
Patricia Boyes appeared by zoom for and with Lawrence Martin (in person) and Jewel Martin (in person).

Mr. Burrey stated that he still has been unable to contact his client and that they are still preparing the financials for the accounting. The court will remove Darlene Daniels as the administrator. The court asked who will be petitioning to be appointed as the successor administrator. Mr. Burrey stated that he does not think a successor administrator is needed, that all that needs to be done is final distribution, that all of the funds have been spent and that he does not see how they will be able to recover any assets for the heirs. Mrs. Boyes stated that she thought the court had ordered the public administrator to oversee the estate back in October, and that Darlene may have given funds to her daughter to buy a house. Status on close of estate is continued to June 26, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The surety company is to be noticed of the removal of the administrator. The court will invite the public administrator to appear on June 26th.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Murphy. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Continued To Dec-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Leighton Burrey, Esq., via Zoom, for Darlene Daniels, Administrator.

Elizabeth Boyes, Esq., via Zoom, for and with Miss Jewel Martin and Lawrence Martin, in court, Objectors.

At the court's query, Mr. Burrey reports that his client is dealing with health issues and has not spoken to her in a few weeks. Ms. Boyes requests the suspension of the Administrator. Mr. Burrey agrees with Ms. Boyes' request to the court. After brief, additional discussion, the court orders the Administrator suspended in open court. The court advises of its intention to invite the San Francisco Administrator (SFPA) to appear at the next calling to possibly take this matter. There is no bond in place therefore notice to a bonding company is not required. Mr. Burrey is advised that he may be ordered to file a Petition for Final Distribution if the SFPA decides not to take the matter.

The subject matters are continued to Dec-13-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cabrera. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar May-23-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Leighton Burrey appeared by zoom for Darlene Daniels (petitioner).
Elizabeth Boyes appeared by zoom for and with Jewel Martin in person and for and with Lawrence Martin in person (objectors).
Lois Woodard appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Mr. Burrey stated that in the amended petition that was field by his client, they wanted the Court to admit the will to probate without having to start all over. Discussion was held regarding the parties knowledge of the will and the will having been lost. The Petition for probate of will and letters for testamentary is taken under submission.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-28-2023 Continued To May-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leighton Burrey appeared by zoom for Darlene Daniels (petitioner).
Elizabeth Boyes appeared by zoom for and with Jewel Martin in person and for and with Lawrence Martin in person (objectors).
Lois Woodard appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court noted that there was a recent filing of a supplemental response to the objections, that it received the petition for probate of lost will on October 25, 2022, and questions when Mr. Burrey's client learned about the purported holographic will. Mr. Burrey stated that his client learned about it sometime in 2021 after the mother passed away, and that she learned of it through Pastor John Jeffrey. The court noted that the time when it received the petition exceeds the 60-day requirement of Probate Code 8226(c)(2), and questions how Mr. Burrey gets around the issue of failure to file it within 60 days. Discussion was held about the allegations that the will was forged in its entirety, the typed written wills that are floating around and are unsigned, the objectors having been given time to rebut/contest the authenticity of the signature, Mrs. Daniels having received the will from Pastor Jeffrey, then losing it and then finding it again, the issue of whether Pastor Jeffrey had capacity to do a declaration under penalty of perjury, whether the objection should be considered as a will contest (PC 8250), the requirement of service of a summons, the parties having met and conferred, Mrs. Boyes not being interested in spending more assets on mediation, a will contest requiring personal service on the heirs, the size of the estate qualifying for pro bono mediation, etc. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to May 2, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Burrey is to file a memo as to why PC 8226(c)(2) does not apply by April 18, 2023. A response is to be filed by April 25, 2023.
Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Malone. Not reported.

Based on the unavailabilty of the Judicial Officer, the matter is continued to May 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The probate examiner will e-mail counsel.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Continued To Mar-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leighton Burrey appeared by zoom for and with Darlene Daniels (petitioner).
Elizabeth Boyes appeared by zoom for and with Jewel Martin (in person) and for and with Lawrence Martin, Jr. (in person).

Mrs. Boyes stated that she filed an objection, that she understands it's a little late because they did not receive notice that the petition had been republished, and that they found out last week by checking the court's docket. Mr. Burrey stated that notice was served, that the presumption of the will being destroyed is inapplicable in this case, that the objection is a regurgitation of what was filed back in November except it adds a copy of an unsigned document purporting to be a will and that the document has all of the hallmarks of fraud. The court noted that the dates on the will that were attached to the objection is different than the date of the holographic will, that there are also other differences between the two, and that something strange is happening which it can't quite figure out. Further discussion was held regarding the need to do discovery, the possibility of doing mediation, the estate not being very large, whether the matter can be decided on the papers, no evidence having been presented, Pastor Jeffrey indicating that the will he has is a holographic will, etc. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to March 28, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If Mrs. Boyes is going to file an amended objection, she is to do so no later than March 21, 2023.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-24-2023 Continued To Feb-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Leighton Burrey, Esq. via Zoom for Darlene Daniels. Elizabeth Boyes, Esq. via Zoom for Jewel Martin and Lawrence Martin (Objectors); both objectors appeared in-person. Lois Woodward (Interested Party) appeared via Zoom.

The Court stated that it had read all of the paperwork and inquired if Ms. Boyes had proof that the handwriting did not belong to the decedent. Ms. Boyes stated that she did not have the proof at this time and that she is requesting time to respond. The Court noted that what's being presented here is a lost will. Mr. Burrey stated that they did file a petition re: lost will and that the petition was served. Ms. Boyes stated that no new publication was on the docket. Brief discussions are held. The clerk informs the Court that there was a publication filed on November 10, 2022. A brief recess is taken by the Court.

The Court takes the bench, all present. The Court stated that the November 10, 2022, proof of publication does not reflect a lost will. Mr. Burrey addressed the petition and service noting that it does not say that it is a lost will. The Court stated that it is not at a point where it will be making any decisions, briefly touched on there being a different probate code, and stated that a key issue will be if the will was left with the pastor. The Court stated that it would continue the matter for Mr. Burrey to re-notice/republish. Mr. Burrey requested a 30-day continuance.

The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to February 28, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Cristobal. Matter not reported.
